title: The remark in the Supreme Court
description: Hearing a matter concerning fraudulent law degrees, Chief Justice of India Surya Kant said: "There are youngsters like cockroaches, who don't get any employment or have any place in the profession. Some of them become media, some of them become social media, RTI activists and other activists, and they start attacking everyone." The remark, made in open court, was reported within days and drew immediate criticism. Kant later clarified that he had meant people practising on fake academic credentials, and that he respected the country's youth.

sources.title: Al Jazeera — Top Indian judge's comment sparks satire, protest

title: A re-exam is announced for the leaked NEET-UG paper
description: The same day, at a press conference in New Delhi, Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan announced that the leaked NEET-UG paper of 3 May - cancelled on 12 May after leak complaints - would be re-conducted on 21 June, with fees refunded and a shift to computer-based testing from the following year. He said the CBI had already begun investigating the breach.

sources.title: Outlook India - NEET UG 2026 re-exam scheduled for June 21, major reforms announced
Moneylife - Govt announces re-exam on 21st June, refunds fees, promises CBT format

statements.speaker: Dharmendra Pradhan
statements.quote: Our approach will remain zero tolerance towards malpractices and anti-social elements. With advancements in technology, such challenges are emerging, but we will not allow any compromise with students' future.
statements.context: At the press conference announcing the 21 June re-exam.

source.title: Outlook India - NEET UG 2026 re-exam scheduled for June 21

statements.speaker: Dharmendra Pradhan
statements.quote: We did not want any deserving student to be deprived of their rights because of the conspiracy of education mafias or because an undeserving candidate benefited through unfair means.
statements.context: Explaining the decision to cancel the leaked 3 May paper; he said the CBI had begun investigating.

source.title: Moneylife - Govt announces re-exam on 21st June

title: The insult is adopted as a name
description: Abhijeet Dipke, a public relations graduate then based in the United States, posted the idea of a "Cockroach Janta Party" the day after the remark and built it out online within about a day — a website carrying the tagline "Voice of the Lazy & Unemployed", plus social accounts. The name deliberately took the slur and wore it.

sources.title: Al Jazeera — Top Indian judge's comment sparks satire, protest
Wikipedia — Cockroach Janta Party

title: Millions join in days
description: The party's Instagram account passed three million followers within roughly 78 hours of launch, and within five days had overtaken the BJP's official account. Sign-ups ran into the hundreds of thousands in the first week. What began as satire had become the fastest-growing political account in the country.

sources.title: Wikipedia — Cockroach Janta Party
FairPlanet — India's Cockroach Revolution

title: The X account is blocked in India
description: The party's account on X was withheld inside India following a blocking order from the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology. The account was restored only after a court intervened seven weeks later.

sources.title: Wikipedia — Cockroach Janta Party

title: The CBSE marking row: 'I take responsibility'
description: After meeting CBSE officials at the board's Dwarka headquarters over the On-Screen Marking evaluation row - the second grievance the movement would later carry - the Education Minister told reporters he took responsibility for fixing it, while defending the OSM system itself as 'a progressive instrument'.

sources.title: The Week - CBSE's OSM system row: 'I take responsibility', says Education Minister

statements.speaker: Dharmendra Pradhan
statements.quote: I take responsibility. It will be fixed; a solution will be found. We are all working on that task.
statements.context: To media after the CBSE meeting at the board's Dwarka headquarters.

source.title: The Week - 'I take responsibility', says Education Minister

title: From the timeline to the street
description: Dipke arrived from the United States and the movement held its first street protest, at Jantar Mantar in New Delhi. Demonstrators gave Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan seven days to resign over the handling of the NEET-UG paper leak and the CBSE marking irregularities.

sources.title: Wikipedia — 2026 Delhi Jantar Mantar protests

title: A month in, and spreading beyond Delhi
description: One month after the remark, the movement had carried its protest from Instagram into the streets of Delhi, Pune, Bengaluru and Lucknow, drawing together anger over exam paper leaks, graduate unemployment and the absence of official accountability.

sources.title: Al Jazeera — A month of India's Cockroach Janta Party

title: The indefinite sit-in begins at Jantar Mantar
description: Demonstrators returned to Jantar Mantar and began an open-ended occupation, banging plates and spoons and again demanding the Education Minister's resignation. Footage from a rally around this period shows a stage banner reading "PEACEFUL PROTEST" naming Dharmendra Pradhan alongside the word "RESIGN".

sources.title: Wikipedia — 2026 Delhi Jantar Mantar protests
AP - India's 'Cockroach Party' protests exam leaks (photos of the day)

title: The NEET-UG re-exam is held
description: Around 22 lakh candidates sat the re-conducted NEET-UG paper across the country, five weeks after the original was cancelled.

sources.title: India TV News - NEET re-exam: Dharmendra Pradhan makes appeal to aspirants

statements.speaker: Dharmendra Pradhan
statements.quote: Today, around 22 lakh students are going to sit for NEET after a short while. They should sit fearlessly and free of anxiety.
statements.context: At an International Yoga Day programme at Delhi University on the morning of the re-exam, where he also asked critics not to 'play with the future of India's new generation'.

source.title: India TV News - Pradhan makes appeal to aspirants

title: Pradhan's first response to the movement
description: In an NDTV interview, the Education Minister spoke about the Cockroach Janta Party directly for the first time, and accused the opposition of frightening students ahead of the re-exam. Outlets differed on the central phrase: Free Press Journal and NDTV carried 'B team of disruptive elements', while The Wire and PA-syndicated reports rendered it as 'B-team of terrorists'.

sources.title: Free Press Journal - 'B Team Of Disruptive Elements': Education Minister breaks silence on Cockroach Janta Party

statements.speaker: Dharmendra Pradhan
statements.quote: They are the B team of disruptive elements. Those who were rejected in the democracy have come in disguise and are now after the system.
statements.context: His first direct response to the Cockroach Janta Party; in the same interview he said the group raises 'slogans for those who want the country to be broken'.

source.title: Free Press Journal - 'B Team Of Disruptive Elements'

statements.speaker: Dharmendra Pradhan
statements.quote: The Opposition has the right to ask questions. But three days before the exam (re-exam), Rahul Gandhi held a programme in Kota to scare students, to derail students' preparations.
statements.context: In the same interview, accusing the Congress of 'low-level politics' over the re-exam.

source.title: Free Press Journal - 'B Team Of Disruptive Elements'

title: Sonam Wangchuk begins a hunger strike
description: The Ladakhi engineer and education reformer Sonam Wangchuk joined the sit-in and began an indefinite hunger strike alongside six students from the All India Students' Association, including its national president Neha Bora.

image.alt: Sonam Wangchuk, cross-legged on a white mattress under a tent, in conversation with CPI(M) leaders Brinda Karat and M. A. Baby seated beside him
image.caption: Wangchuk on the second day of his fast, with CPI(M) leaders M. A. Baby and Brinda Karat at Jantar Mantar, 29 June.

sources.title: Wikipedia — 2026 Delhi Jantar Mantar protests

title: "Pradhan Go Back" goes national
description: The party launched a nationwide campaign calling for the Education Minister's removal, carrying the protest well beyond the Delhi sit-in.

sources.title: Wikipedia — Cockroach Janta Party

title: Delhi High Court orders the account unblocked
description: The Delhi High Court directed that the party's withheld X account be restored, ending the block imposed in May.

sources.title: Wikipedia — Cockroach Janta Party

title: Wangchuk is removed from the protest site
description: On the twentieth day of his fast, Delhi Police forcibly removed Sonam Wangchuk from Jantar Mantar and admitted him to Safdarjung Hospital. Abhijeet Dipke began an indefinite hunger strike in response, joined by Students' Federation of India leader Adarsh M. Saji and activist Aishe Ghosh. Footage from the site shows participants sitting among books under tarpaulin shelters, a banner reading "HUNGER STRIKE DAY 20", and a placard invoking Bhagat Singh's 1929 jail protest.

sources.title: Wikipedia — 2026 Delhi Jantar Mantar protests

statements.speaker: DCP New Delhi
statements.role: Official X account, Deputy Commissioner of Police, New Delhi district
statements.quote: As per orders of Hon'ble High Court and on expert medical advise due to the deteriorating health condition of Sh. Sonam Wangchuk, he has been shifted to the hospital for essential medical care.
statements.context: Posted hours after the removal ('advise' as in the original). The post went on to say protesters 'tried to create obstruction', that 'slight commotion ensued', that police showed 'maximum restrain', and asked protesters to 'peacefully vacate' Jantar Mantar.

source.title: DCP New Delhi on X

statements.speaker: Unnamed senior officer, Delhi Police
statements.role: Senior officer speaking to media; not named by the outlet
statements.quote: We didn't attack them or hit them. We didn't even cut off electricity. All their allegations are false. We only picked up Sonam because of doctor's advice who told us he is suffering from severe dehydration
statements.context: Responding to protesters' allegations that officers shoved and beat people during the pre-dawn removal.

source.title: Outlook India - Delhi Police declare CJP's July 20 'Sansad Chalo' march illegal

title: Chalo Sansad — the march on Parliament
description: On the opening day of the monsoon session, the movement marched on Parliament. Thousands of demonstrators moved along the approach roads carrying Indian flags; Al Jazeera and NPR both put the crowd in the tens of thousands. Police had declined permission for the march.

image.alt: A dense protest crowd at Jantar Mantar; in the foreground a man carries a bronze statue of Dr. B. R. Ambedkar on his shoulder, surrounded by flags, banners and placards
image.caption: The staging ground at Jantar Mantar on the morning of 20 July, hours before the march set off for Parliament.
image.credit: Photo: Sumita Roy Dutta, CC BY-SA 4.0, via Wikimedia Commons

sources.title: Al Jazeera — Police attack Cockroach activists as thousands march on Indian parliament
NPR — India's Gen Z 'Cockroach People's Party'

statements.speaker: Delhi Police
statements.quote: Prohibitory orders u/s 163 BNSS are in force in New Delhi District except designated protest site at Jantar Mantar Road. Further, no permission has been sought or granted for any proposed march to Parliament on 20 July 2026.
statements.context: The formal permission refusal, posted the day before the march; the advisory added that 'Citizens are advised not to participate in any unauthorised gathering or procession'.

source.title: The Logical Indian - Delhi Police invokes Section 163 BNSS ahead of 'Chalo Sansad' march

statements.speaker: Narendra Modi
statements.quote: Be it the monsoon or the monsoon session, if both are proactive, they prove to be highly productive.
statements.context: From the customary address to media before the session opened, hours before the march reached the barricades. The address made no reference to the protests, the NEET dispute or the movement.

source.title: India TV News - Monsoon Session 2026 live updates (20 July)

title: Tear gas, lathi charges and mass detentions
description: Police fired tear gas and carried out baton charges against the crowd. Uniformed personnel are filmed taking hold of individuals at a metal barricade and swinging a cane. Dozens of protesters and more than a hundred police personnel were reported injured, dozens of activists were detained including Samajwadi Party MP Dimple Yadav, and internet access was shut down in the area around Parliament. Whether Dipke himself was detained was disputed within hours: Delhi Police formally denied it mid-march, while evening reports listed him among those held. Separately, men in civilian dress were filmed carrying lathis alongside uniformed officers; the framing of that footage is disputed.

sources.title: Al Jazeera — Police attack Cockroach activists as thousands march on Indian parliament
Al Jazeera — Photos: Police fire tear gas at India's Cockroach movement supporters
Newslaundry - Lathis, tear gas and injured protesters: the crackdown in pictures
Wikipedia — 2026 Delhi Jantar Mantar protests

statements.speaker: Delhi Police
statements.quote: Some segments from media have mentioned sporadic use of violence/detentions at Jantar Mantar by Delhi Police. It is informed that no such incident has taken place and protest is being handled professionally.
statements.context: Posted mid-afternoon, while lathi charges and tear gas were being reported from the ground. The same night, the force's own statement acknowledged more than 118 injured personnel.

source.title: BOOM - Delhi Police calls real CJP protest video 'fake', denies brutality

statements.speaker: Delhi Police
statements.quote: Misleading reports are currently circulating on various social media platforms claiming that Abhijit Dipke has been detained by the Delhi Police. It is formally clarified that these allegations are entirely false and he is available on stage.
statements.context: Issued mid-march after CJP spokesperson Saurav Das claimed Dipke had been detained; Das retracted the claim. Evening reports nonetheless listed Dipke among those held.

source.title: IBTimes India - Dipke not arrested; Das backtracks; SP MP Dimple Yadav detained

statements.speaker: Delhi Police
statements.quote: During today's violent protests, more than 118 police personnel from Delhi Police and Central Police Forces, including Special Commissioner, Joint Commissioner, Additional Commissioner, and Deputy Commissioner rank officers deployed on duty, sustained injuries.
statements.context: The force's injury count, posted on the evening of the clashes as Commissioner Anurag Kumar visited hospitalised officers.

source.title: IANS Live - Over 118 police personnel injured in violent Delhi protest clashes

statements.speaker: Delhi Police
statements.quote: The protesters attacked police personnel with stones and other objects, attempted to breach police barricades, vandalised police and other government vehicles, damaged public property and resorted to large-scale violence.
statements.context: From the force's late-night statement, which put police injuries at more than 118, protester injuries at around 60, detentions at around 70, and announced an FIR for rioting and assault on public servants.

source.title: Telangana Today - Violent CJP protest leaves 118 police personnel injured, 70 detained

title: CJP delegates meet J.P. Nadda
description: After the government reached out that morning, CJP spokespersons Sourav Das and Ashutosh Ranka met Union Health Minister J.P. Nadda in New Delhi - the movement had refused meetings with a district magistrate, a union secretary and a minister of state, insisting on a Cabinet minister. The demands presented included the resignations of the Education Minister and the Prime Minister, national examination reforms, and compensation and justice for the families of students who died by suicide. No outcome was reported.

sources.title: Outlook India - CJP leaders Sourav Das, Ashutosh Ranka meet JP Nadda over NEET row

title: The site is rebuilt
description: Protesters returned to Jantar Mantar and rebuilt the dismantled protest site, as disputes over the previous day's footage and injury figures played out in public.

image.alt: The Jantar Mantar avenue packed with thousands of protesters between rebuilt tarpaulin shelters, tricolour flags flying, protesters in the foreground with arms raised
image.caption: 'Jantar Mantar Is Again Packed!' - the site on the morning of 21 July, as posted by the movement's own account.

sources.title: Wikipedia — 2026 Delhi Jantar Mantar protests

statements.speaker: Delhi Police
statements.quote: Reports claiming that police forces are using pellet guns against peaceful protesters are completely false and misleading.
statements.context: A 'Fake News Alert' posted after CJP spokesperson Saurav Das alleged pellet-gun use during the crackdown; the post warned of legal action against those spreading rumours.

source.title: Siasat Daily - Delhi Police denies CJP's pellet gun claims

statements.speaker: Unnamed senior officer, Delhi Police
statements.role: Senior officer speaking to ThePrint; not named
statements.quote: This is not illegal deployment. However, they are deployed in law and order duties as reinforcements during situations such as yesterday's, and it was neither illegal nor unprecedented.
statements.context: On videos of plainclothes men wielding lathis alongside uniformed officers on 20 July; the officer said they were policemen from 'the special staff and from the teams which are not part of active policing duties'.

source.title: ThePrint - Lathi-wielding plainclothes cops spark row; police say deployment lawful

title: The High Court moves Wangchuk to Medanta
description: In Gitanjali Angmo v. Union of India, the Delhi High Court allowed the hunger-striking Sonam Wangchuk to be shifted from the government-run Safdarjung Hospital to Medanta Hospital in Gurugram. After hearing the doctors present in court, the bench said there was a consensus that constant monitoring was needed.

sources.title: Bar and Bench - Delhi High Court allows Sonam Wangchuk to be shifted to Medanta Hospital

title: The CJP halts street marches
description: The movement announced it would stop marching through New Delhi's streets out of concern for supporters' safety, while continuing the Jantar Mantar sit-in and its campaign. 'We will not march again because the police will hurt the youth again,' founder Abhijeet Dipke said. Delhi police put the injured from 20 July at nearly 180 - 118 security personnel and about 60 protesters - against the CJP's own count of 150 protesters wounded, and said legal action would be initiated against 70 detained protesters.

sources.title: Al Jazeera - India's 'cockroach' protest movement halts marches after police violence

title: Parliament stalls; opposition leaders detained outside the PM's residence
description: Both Houses were repeatedly adjourned and then adjourned for the day as opposition MPs demanded a discussion of the NEET-UG leak and the police action, and the Education Minister's resignation. In the afternoon, Rahul Gandhi, Priyanka Gandhi Vadra, Akhilesh Yadav and other opposition leaders were detained during a sit-in near the Prime Minister's residence at Lok Kalyan Marg, and released hours later. Left MPs demanded that Home Minister Amit Shah come to Parliament and explain the police action; through 22 July Shah had made no public statement on the crackdown, with The Tribune reporting - via unnamed sources - that he had taken personal charge of the situation and convened a meeting of ministers.

image.alt: Rahul Gandhi in a white polo shirt, held by the arm by a Delhi Police officer amid a crush of people and press cameras
image.caption: Rahul Gandhi during the detention outside the Prime Minister's residence, in a photo published on his own account after release.

sources.title: Republic World - Parliament Monsoon Session live updates (21 July)
Onmanorama - Rahul, Priyanka Gandhi detained after sit-in outside PM Modi's residence
Outlook India - Left MPs demand Amit Shah's statement in Parliament over police action
The Tribune - Government ready to discuss exam reforms; Amit Shah reviews situation

title: Modi's first response, behind closed doors
description: The Prime Minister's first reported response to the movement came at a closed-door NDA parliamentary party meeting in the Parliament Library Building, relayed to reporters afterwards by Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju - not delivered in public or on the floor of either House. Per that account, he called the NEET-UG paper leak a 'grave sin', said the government stood with students, and appealed to leaders not to incite the young.

sources.title: Oneindia - PM Modi breaks silence on NEET-UG protest, urges NDA MPs to guide youth
Medical Dialogues - NEET paper leak a 'grave sin': PM Modi says govt stands with students

statements.speaker: Narendra Modi
statements.quote: It is easy to go on the wrong path, but it is much tougher to return to the right path. We have to bring them onto the right path. That is our responsibility.
statements.context: As relayed to reporters by Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju from the closed-door NDA meeting - a second-hand account, not a transcript.

source.title: Oneindia - PM Modi breaks silence on NEET-UG protest

statements.speaker: Narendra Modi
statements.quote: Do not incite them, show them the right path.
statements.context: An appeal aimed at leaders he said were misleading protesting youth, from the same relayed account; outlets rendered the line with slight variations.

source.title: Medical Dialogues - NEET paper leak a 'grave sin'

title: Pradhan breaks his silence, on X
description: Late that night, the Education Minister posted his first detailed public response to the weeks of protests centring on him - on X, not in Parliament. The thread attacked the Congress leadership over the day's dharna, said the government was ready to discuss NEET in the House, and gave no indication that he intends to resign.

sources.title: The Wire - Responding to protests centring on him, Pradhan attacks Congress, gives no sign of resignation
India TV News - 'Committed to discussing NEET, owe students more than outrage'

statements.speaker: Dharmendra Pradhan
statements.quote: LoP Shri Rahul Gandhi and INC continue to shamelessly exploit students as political tools to manufacture disruption during the Monsoon Session of Parliament
statements.context: From the X thread posted after Congress leaders' dharna outside the Prime Minister's residence.

source.title: India TV News - Pradhan hits out at Rahul Gandhi

statements.speaker: Dharmendra Pradhan
statements.quote: They deserve certainty over chaos, solutions over slogans and responsibility over disruption. We owe our students more than outrage. We owe them answers, reforms and accountability.
statements.context: The Wire reported the thread as his first exhaustive remark on the protests, noting he gave no indication he intends to resign.

source.title: The Wire - Pradhan attacks Congress, gives no sign of resignation

statements.speaker: Dharmendra Pradhan
statements.quote: Our Government remains 100% committed to discussing NEET and addressing every genuine concern of our youth on the floor of the house.
statements.context: Posted after the government conveyed readiness for a Lok Sabha discussion on the paper leak.

source.title: India TV News - 'Committed to discussing NEET'

title: Wangchuk in intensive care; ministers visit
description: On the 24th day of his fast, Sonam Wangchuk was in the Intensive Care Unit at Medanta Hospital in Gurugram, where Union Ministers J.P. Nadda and Jitendra Singh visited him. Hospital officials said he intended to continue the hunger strike until protesting student leaders were allowed to meet MPs at Parliament House, or parliamentarians visited him. Solidarity protests were reported in New York, San Jose, London and Dublin.

sources.title: The Quint - Union Ministers JP Nadda, Jitendra Singh visit Sonam Wangchuk in hospital

title: The government agrees to a Lok Sabha discussion
description: Amid a third day of disruption, Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju told the Lok Sabha the government was ready to discuss the NEET paper leak, with the format to be decided by the Speaker after meeting floor leaders. The opposition held to its demand: 'Our demand is very clear: Education Minister should resign,' Congress's K.C. Venugopal said, while Akhilesh Yadav said the issue 'is not of Congress, Samajwadi Party or the BJP; it's about the youth of the country'.

sources.title: Prokerala (IANS) - LS erupts over NEET row; Oppn adamant on Pradhan's resignation

title: Talks stall over a venue; the fast goes on
description: A further meeting between the CJP and J.P. Nadda was expected but stalled: chief spokesperson Saurav Das said the party would not travel to Nadda's office, and that any meeting should be held at a neutral venue near Jantar Mantar. The CJP publicly appealed to Wangchuk to end his hunger strike - 'Your sacrifice has inspired a generation' - as opposition MPs visited Medanta urging the same, and police in Patna used tear gas and water cannon on AISA workers protesting over the leak. As of mid-afternoon, no meeting had taken place.

sources.title: Deccan Chronicle live blog - CJP seeks neutral venue for Nadda meeting
